message:
fix: close reactor overflow, shutdown race, timer testability, and cancel priority semantics

Reactor events: enforce capacity limit in push() by dropping events when
full, preventing unbounded memory growth under sustained event storms.
The previous approach assumed the reactor's poll batch size would
naturally cap the buffer, which is not guaranteed under burst conditions.

Connection manager: double-check shutdown signal after acquiring the
state lock to close the TOCTOU race where shutdown begins between the
initial phase check and the actual connection registration, preventing
new connections from being accepted during shutdown.

Timer wheels: tick() now takes an explicit Instant parameter instead of
calling Instant::now() internally, making the timer deterministically
testable and avoiding multiple now() calls within a single tick cascade.
Hierarchical wheel's advance_to also passes the caller-provided instant
through to tick(). Timer wheel next-tick arithmetic uses saturating_add
to prevent overflow on extreme tick distances.

Cancel budget tests: corrected priority combination assertions to match
the actual Budget::combine semantics where priority takes the minimum
(most restrictive) value, not the maximum. Shutdown priority test updated
to assert min-preserving behavior rather than hardcoded 255.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
